title: Enrichment Explanation (themes format)
description: LLM-generated structured explanation of GO enrichment output in depth-anchor themes format. Fill every field strictly from the input enrichment data provided — do not introduce gene symbols or GO IDs that are not present in the input.

themes.description: One explanation object per theme in the input, in the same order as the input themes array.

theme_index.description: 0-based index matching the position of this theme in the input themes array.

narrative.description: 2-3 sentence biological narrative explaining what this theme means and why these genes cluster together. Tag EVERY sentence with exactly one of: [DATA] for statements derived directly from the enrichment numbers, [GO-HIERARCHY] for statements about how GO terms relate to each other, [INFERENCE] for mechanistic or biological interpretations that go beyond the raw numbers, or [EXTERNAL] for claims that require knowledge external to the enrichment data (e.g. known biology from the literature). Use only genes and GO terms from this theme's input data.

key_insights.description: 2-4 key biological insights about this theme. Each insight must reference a specific GO term from this theme's input data.

insight.description: One specific biological insight. Be concrete — reference gene names or processes from the input data.

go_id.description: The GO term ID most relevant to this insight. MUST be one of the GO IDs present in this theme's input data (anchor or specific terms).

key_genes.description: 2-5 key genes for this theme. You MUST select only from the candidate_key_genes list provided in the input for this theme — do not introduce genes from external knowledge.

gene.description: Gene symbol. MUST appear in the candidate_key_genes list for this theme.

go_id.description: The specific GO term in this theme that most directly involves this gene. MUST be from this theme's input data.

description.description: One sentence explaining this gene's role in the context of this theme. If claim_type is EXTERNAL, this may draw on known biology; if INFERENCE, it should be grounded in the enrichment evidence.

claim_type.description: EXTERNAL if the gene description relies on prior biological knowledge; INFERENCE if it is derived from the enrichment data alone.

statistical_context.description: One sentence summarising the statistical evidence for this theme: include the anchor term FDR, fold enrichment, and gene count. Use the numbers directly from the input — do not round or approximate.

hub_genes.description: Explanations for hub genes — genes that appear across 3 or more themes. Only include genes that are listed as hub_genes in the input data.

gene.description: Gene symbol. MUST be a gene listed in the input hub_genes section.

narrative.description: 1-2 sentences explaining why this gene appears across multiple themes and what that cross-theme role implies biologically.

claim_type.description: EXTERNAL if the narrative relies on prior biological knowledge; INFERENCE if derived from the enrichment patterns alone.

overall_summary.description: 3-4 paragraph overall biological narrative synthesising all themes into a coherent story. Each element of this array is one paragraph (a string). Do not include sub-headings — write continuous prose. The final paragraph should address any limitations or caveats.
